ReportText:
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

Specimen: Endometrial biopsy (Pipelle)

DIAGNOSIS: Endometrioid adenocarcinoma, FIGO Grade 3

Microscopic Description:
Fragments of endometrial tissue with markedly atypical glands showing solid growth pattern (>50% solid). High nuclear grade with prominent nucleoli and frequent mitotic figures (>10/10 HPF). Focal areas of squamous differentiation. No definite myometrial tissue for invasion assessment. Background atrophic endometrium.

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
- ER: Positive, 40% of tumor cells, moderate intensity
- PR: Positive, 10% of tumor cells, weak intensity
- p53: Wild-type pattern (heterogeneous staining)
- p16: Patchy positive (not block positive)

MISMATCH REPAIR PROTEIN IHC:
- MLH1: LOSS of nuclear expression in tumor cells (intact in stroma)
- PMS2: LOSS of nuclear expression in tumor cells (intact in stroma)
- MSH2: INTACT nuclear expression
- MSH6: INTACT nuclear expression

Interpretation: Loss of MLH1 and PMS2 expression suggesting mismatch repair deficiency (dMMR). This pattern may be due to Lynch syndrome (germline MLH1 mutation) or MLH1 promoter hypermethylation. Clinical correlation and further testing recommended.

Comment: Given grade 3 histology and MMR deficiency, further molecular testing including POLE exonuclease domain sequencing is recommended for 2023 FIGO molecular-integrated staging.

SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT - SYNOPTIC

Specimen: Uterus, cervix, bilateral fallopian tubes, bilateral ovaries, sentinel lymph nodes

SYNOPTIC REPORT — ENDOMETRIAL CARCINOMA (CAP Protocol)

Procedure: Total hysterectomy with bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy

TUMOR:
  Histologic Type: Endometrioid adenocarcinoma
  Histologic Grade (FIGO): Grade 3 (>50% solid growth)
  Tumor Size: 3.8 x 3.2 x 2.1 cm
  Myometrial Invasion: Present
    Depth of invasion: 14 mm
    Myometrial thickness: 22 mm
    Percent invasion: 64% (>50% = deep invasion)
  Uterine Serosa: Not involved
  Cervical Involvement:
    Endocervical glandular involvement: Absent
    Cervical stromal invasion: Absent
  Lymphovascular Space Invasion: PRESENT (extensive, >5 foci)
  Parametrial Involvement: Absent

MARGINS:
  Vaginal cuff margin: Negative (>10 mm)
  Cervical margin: Negative
  All other margins: Negative

LYMPH NODES:
  Left pelvic sentinel nodes: 0/2 (negative, including ultrastaging)
  Right pelvic sentinel node: 0/1 (negative, including ultrastaging)
  Total: 0/3 positive
  Isolated tumor cells: Not identified

ADDITIONAL FINDINGS:
  Right ovary: Benign serous cystadenoma (1.5 cm), no malignancy
  Left ovary: Unremarkable, no malignancy
  Bilateral fallopian tubes: No significant pathology, no STIC lesions
  Peritoneal washings: Negative for malignancy
  Background endometrium: Atrophic

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (confirmed on hysterectomy specimen):
  ER: Positive, 35% tumor cells
  PR: Positive, 5% tumor cells
  p53: Wild-type pattern
  MLH1: LOSS
  PMS2: LOSS
  MSH2: Intact
  MSH6: Intact
  HER2: 1+ (negative)
  Ki-67: 45%
  L1CAM: Positive (>10% membranous staining)

MOLECULAR TESTING:
  POLE exonuclease domain: No pathogenic mutation detected (wild-type)
  MSI testing (PCR): MSI-High (5/5 markers unstable)
  MLH1 promoter methylation: NEGATIVE (not methylated)
  TMB: 42 mutations/Mb (high)

MOLECULAR CLASSIFICATION (2023 FIGO / ProMisE):
  POLEmut: No
  MMRd: YES (MLH1/PMS2 loss, MSI-H, germline MLH1 confirmed)
  p53abn: No (wild-type p53)
  NSMP: N/A (MMRd identified)
  → Classification: MMRd

PATHOLOGIC STAGING (FIGO 2023):
  pT1b: Tumor invades >50% of myometrium
  pN0(sn): No regional lymph node metastasis (sentinel)
  Stage: IB
  Molecular-integrated stage: IBm-MMRd

ADDITIONAL MOLECULAR FINDINGS (NGS Panel):
  PIK3CA: c.3140A>G (p.H1047R) - Pathogenic (hotspot mutation)
  PTEN: c.388C>T (p.R130*) - Pathogenic (truncating)
  ARID1A: c.5548C>T (p.R1850*) - Pathogenic (truncating)
  KRAS: Wild-type
  CTNNB1: Wild-type
  NTRK1/2/3 fusions: Not detected
  HRD score: Not applicable (not assessed)
